methodology.description: Conversion rates derived from industry-specific overall rate applied to traffic source base rates

source.title: eCommerce Statistics and Benchmarks by Industry - XP2
source.snippet: Food & Beverage: 6.02% overall conversion rate - highest of all industries

derivation.method: Industry overall rate × paid traffic base ratio

conv_paid.notes: Highest converting category; consumable nature drives purchase intent

source.title: eCommerce Statistics and Benchmarks by Industry - XP2
source.snippet: Food & Beverage: 6.02% overall conversion rate - highest of all industries

derivation.method: Industry overall rate × organic traffic base ratio

conv_organic.notes: Recipe/content-driven organic traffic converts exceptionally well

source.title: eCommerce Statistics and Benchmarks by Industry - XP2
source.snippet: Food & Beverage: 6.02% overall conversion rate - highest of all industries

derivation.method: Industry overall rate × email/retention traffic base ratio

conv_retention.notes: Highest repeat purchase category; subscription models common

source.title: Customer Acquisition Cost Benchmarks by Industry

cac_paid.notes: Lower CAC justified by high LTV from repeat purchases

source.title: Average Order Value by Industry
source.snippet: Food and beverage: $114 AOV (full market); DTC subscription typically $55-80

aov_typical.notes: Lower ticket but high frequency; subscriptions increase effective AOV

source.title: Understanding Margins and Revenue in DTC Ecommerce

gross_margin_typical.notes: Perishability, cold chain, and compliance reduce margins
